rulings.Case Title: PANDA RESTAURANT GROUP, INC. et al VS. PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Dismiss; Notice Of Lodgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 13, 2011, Line 4, DEFENDANTS PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN, LLP AND PAULA WEBER'S MOTION To Dismiss IS Denied. This is not one of those ¿rarest of cases¿ where due process requires dismissal. While some of defendants¿ causation defense theories (most notably the theory that plaintiff relied on advice of its own counsel, not on defendants¿ advice) are likely made harder to develop and/or establish due to plaintiff¿s invocation of privilege, that is an insufficient basis to require dismissal. Plaintiff¿s invocation of the privilege does not impede defendants from pursuing a variety of possible defenses including causation defense theories (most notably defendants did not provide advice that could reasonably have been relied on) and thus the drastic remedy of dismissal is inappropriate, particularly since defendants are unimpeded in their use of what they said or was said to them. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HEK

rulings.Case Title: VERONICA TEDESCHI VS. PHILLIP GARDELLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 13, 2011, Line 15, DEFENDANTS ELLEN GARDELLE DBA BISTRO CHAPEAU AND PHILLIPE GARDELLE'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Denied. Defendants have until Janaury 5, 2012 to filed a responsive pleading to the third amended complaint. Setting aside the dispute whether plaintiff sought to file her third amended complaint prior to the date it should have been filed, the strong policy of California law favoring resolution of disputes on their merits and the lack of any discernable prejudice to defendants requires that plaintiff be allowed to proceed with her claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hek

rulings.Case Title: SHAWN CHOY et al VS. LIVE SF ENTERTAINMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Default On Vincent Matteucci
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 13, 2011, Line 5, PLAINTIFFs SHAWN CHOY and HYON CHA's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Default On Vincent Matteucci Denied. The motion effectively seeks reconsideration of the denial of the August 5 ex parte application without presenting any new law, facts or circumstances and more than ten days beyond the denial. Even if the court considered the motion on its merits, it would be denied due to failure to serve Mr. Matteuci with the motion papers. Plaintiffs¿ remedy is to serve Mr. Matteuci with the second amended complaint or re-file the motion with a proof of service on Mr. Matteuci. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hek

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK. N.A., A NATIONAL BANKING VS. TITO P. DE LEON JR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Reliev From Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 13, 2011, Line 16, DEFENDANT TITO DE LEON's MOTION For RelievE From Default Judgment IS Denied. The motion is untimely because it was filed more than six months after entry of the judgment sought to be set aside. Defendant¿s effort to seek relief under some rule of law other than Code of Civil Procedure 473(b) fails since his notice of motion expressly invokes that subsection and no other legal basis for the relief sought. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: THE NINTH AND MISSION CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, A PUBLIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 13, 2011, Line 12, DEFENDANT ENCORE BANK, N.A. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled with ten days to answer. The face of the complaint does not show that the declaratory relief claim is barred as to rental payments received by the Trustee within four years of filing the complaint and until the property is transferred to the District. Ninth and Mission adequately alleges a claim for decretory relief (i.e. a claim that under the Trust Agreement, as to which it is a party, BPG is entitled to rental payments received by the Trustee). Foreclosure of the property does not eliminate Ninth and Mission as a party to the Trust Agreement or otherwise deprive it of standing to seek declaratory relief. BPG adequately alleges standing as an entity who is ¿interested¿ in the payments required to be made to it under the Trust Agreement. The correct interpretation of the Trust Agreement is not an issue which should be resolved on a demurrer, at least where, as here, there are reasonable disputes about that interpretation and the court would need to consider evidence to determine the correct interpretation. (Fremont Indemnity Corporation v. Fremont General Corporaton (2007) 148 Cal. App. 4th 97, 112-18). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/HEK
